title: Sarcochilus centipeda
taxonomy: Sarcochilus centipeda is a species of orchid, and it belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This family is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. The genus Sarcochilus, to which S. centipeda belongs, is native to Australia and New Guinea and comprises about 20 species of orchids. The species S. centipeda is further classified under the subfamily Orchidoideae. The exact classification of S. centipeda within the Orchidaceae family may vary depending on the source.

Sarcochilus centipeda has been used by indigenous Australians for its purported anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots and leaves are often used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and wounds. However, its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied or proven. intro: Sarcochilus centipeda is a type of orchid commonly found in Australia and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Sarcochilus centipeda include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
